results.title: Hydroinformatics Engineer | Jobflarely
highlights: the people who make water work every day. As a leading water technology company with 23,000 employees operating in over 150 countries, Xylem is at the forefront of addressing the world's most critical water challenges. We invite passionate individuals to join our team, dedicated to exceeding customer expectations through innovative and sustainable solutions. We’re Hiring for a Hydroinformatics Engineer ! If you are excited and passionate about helping #LetsSolveWater, consider joining our team today! Xylem, Inc. is a leading global water technology company servicing more than 150 countries and is dedicated to solving the world’s most challenging water issues. We are looking for individuals to join our mission by exceeding customer expectations through smart sustainable solutions. At Xylem, you will have the opportunity to solve water by participating in our paid Volunteer Program, Xylem Watermark ! THE ROLE : The Hydroinformatics Engineer will be responsible for the development and implementation of algorithms and analytics for operational optimization of water distribution, wastewater collection, and treatment systems through the use of real time data, hydraulic modelling, machine learning, and advanced control systems. A Hydroinformatics Engineer is a multidisciplinary engineer with optimization and hydraulic modeling skills and a passion for using data to improve urban watershed environmental and societal impact. This position offers the opportunity to join Xylem Vue team in their endeavor to revolutionize the ways water utilities solve age-old infrastructure problems. In today’s rapidly changing digital solutions landscape, water utilities are trusting Xylem to lead them in their smart water journey; earning this trust begins with excellent software products, and delivery processes that embody the highest levels of technical integrity. The successful candidate will demonstrate how past experiences and education, combined with a passion for improving our industry through digital services, will lead to a highly motivated water project delivery team focused on client success. We offer a full benefits package to include Flexible Time Off (FTO) for salaried positions, health, dental, vision, investment savings plan, and additional miscellaneous benefits. CORE RESPONSIBILITIES: To perform the job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. Act as the subject matter expert to configure, calibrate and deploy digital twin simulation services for optimizing the operation of water and wastewater networks. Solve complicated, open-ended water resources problems by applying optimization techniques and innovative data analytics over sensor data and hydraulic models. Communicate and collaborate with teams of engineers and decision makers to define problems, strategize solution approaches, and bring solutions to implementation. Develop and manage large datasets. Communicate summaries and conclusions drawn from data in clear and concise ways, visually and verbally. Translate data analytics into language understood by engineers and data analysts, both in the practitioner and research space Participation in Xylem Watermark volunteer activities MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS : Bachelor’s Degree in Civil/Environmental/Chemical/Mechanical Engineering; preference given to those with advanced technical degrees or training. Minimum of 3 years of experience in water, sewer or stormwater modelling. Hydraulic modeling software for drinking water networks (EPANET or similar) and (or) stormwater networks (SWMM or similar). Previous experience programming in software languages such as Python, R, C, MATLAB, etc. Experience with modern data science workflows for data access and filtering, geospatial analysis, and model parameter estimation. Excellent verbal and written communication skills. Self-motivated, resourceful, and eager to develop new skills. A creative mind and hands-on problem-solving abilities.

results.title: Ye Li
highlights:
Manager, Data Science Hydroinformatics at [Xylem](https://www.linkedin.com/company/xylem-inc-)
...
Data scientist with a deep expertise in machine learning, anomaly detection, and time-series forecasting, specializing in transforming complex data into actionable insights. My experience spans building advanced AI-driven models for anomaly detection, predictive maintenance, and operational optimization. I have developed Variational Autoencoders (VAEs) for event classification, Seq2Seq models for forecasting, and Bayesian frameworks for adaptive anomaly detection—each delivering high-accuracy results and reducing false alarms significantly. I have successfully led cross-functional teams, mentoring data scientists and driving innovation in AI applications. My commitment to advancing the field extends to academia, where I have co-supervised three PhD students in the EDB-IPP program, providing strategic guidance and technical expertise to shape cutting-edge research.
...
#### Manager, Data Science Hydroinformatics (Current)
...
1. Led and mentored a team of five data scientists, driving the development and delivery of innovative digital solutions across the Southeast Asia region. 2. Effectively coordinated cross-functional teams manage and deliver data science projects across multiple regions, including Australia, Singapore, China, and Europe, ensuring successful execution and alignment with business objectives. 3. Co-supervised three PhD candidates and one master's student under the EDB-IPP program, providing guidance and technical expertise support their academic and research endeavors. Show less
...
#### Senior Data Scientist (Current)
...
1. Developed a data-driven anomaly detection system for identifying water pipe burst events by analysing pressure sensor time-series data, , reducing false alarms by 95% while capturing all true events. 2. Developed an anomaly detection system by using Bayesian frameworks to model dynamic changes in streaming data. Incorporating conjugate exponential models ensures modularity and efficient parameter estimation, enabling adaptive and robust anomaly detection. 3. An Autoencoder model was utilized to learn a lower-dimensional latent representation from raw audio data, capturing essential features for valve fault detection. Show less
...
1. Designed and implemented a robust ETL tool for seamless data retrieval from AWS S3, streamlining data extraction workflows and enhancing operational efficiency across various projects. 2. Developed advanced acoustic leak detection methods by applying generalized cross-correlation, significantly enhancing early leak identification in water distribution networks. Designed and implemented an acoustic localization algorithm using hydrophones, achieving an accuracy of ±3 meters within an 800-meter range, enabling faster repairs and minimizing water loss. 3. Developed advanced sensor health monitoring techniques for hydrophones, pressure sensors, and water quality sensors using sophisticated signal processing methods, ensuring long-term sensor performance and reliability. results.title: Nicolas Swanson
highlights:
Global Engineering Lead, Automation & AI at [Xylem](https://www.linkedin.com/company/xylem-inc-)
...
A seasoned professional with 10 years of immersion in the water industry, Nick blends technical engineering expertise with business ingenuity. As the current Global Engineering Lead, Automation & AI at Xylem, Nick directs an enterprise software team responsible for delivering data-driven AI & automation solutions to facilitate the design and execution of Xylem's product lines. In his time as a Product Manager at Evoqua, Nick led cross-functional, international teams to deliver customer-focused water & wastewater solutions and services to expand market reach. As a Product Engineer, Nick strategized both quality enhancements and cost avoidances for the production of various wastewater and drinking water product lines. Nick earned a B.S. in Chemical Engineering and a Minor in Business Administration from the University of Florida. He is a licensed Professional Engineer in the State of Florida, a certified Azure AI Engineer Associate, and previously held ASQ Certified Six Sigma Black Belt (CSSBB) and SAFe Scrum Master certifications. As a native Floridian, he enjoys his downtime with tropical permaculture gardening, long walks, and reading business & finance books.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) is arguably the most extensively discussed topic in the realm of technology today. Among its many applications, the field of healthcare stands out as one where AI has demonstrated exceptional effectiveness. From predicting diseases to identifying failures in medical equipment, AI has proven to be an invaluable tool for healthcare professionals. Its ability to analyze vast amounts of data quickly and accurately enhances diagnostic accuracy, enables personalized treatment plans, and improves patient outcomes. Looking ahead to the next decade, AI is anticipated to become even more integral to healthcare, offering unprecedented benefits such as advanced predictive analytics and preventive measures. These advancements promise to revolutionize the field, potentially saving more lives than ever before and significantly improving overall public health.

results.title: Business Operations & Data Analyst Intern | Joblume
highlights:
Business Operations & Data Analyst Intern | Joblume
...
# Business Operations & Data Analyst Intern
...
Posted Jun 18, 2026
...
The Business Operations & Data Analyst Intern will support operational improvement initiatives related to an upcoming Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system migration . The intern will assist with developing training and process documentation, organizing operational knowledge resources, preparing and analyzing data, and identifying opportunities to leverage digital tools such as Excel, Power BI, and Microsoft Copilot to improve team effectiveness and enhance the customer experience. This role provides hands-on experience supporting ERP readiness, data quality, process improvement, and digital enablement within a global organization focused on solving critical water challenges. Xylem Summer Internships typically last 10–12 weeks and offer undergraduate students meaningful, hands-on professional experience while making a lasting contribution to the organization. Our internship program is designed to create a pipeline of early-career talent interested in building a career in the water innovation and technology industry . Interns are our primary talent pool for future full-time and rotational opportunities.

We are currently looking for an AI / ML Engineer in the United States.This role sits at the intersection of machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making, focused on solving complex challenges in the energy and utility sector. You will contribute to the development of advanced AI and ML solutions that combine predictive modeling, optimization, and generative AI to support critical infrastructure and sustainability goals. Working closely with data scientists and software engineers, you will help design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems that power real-world applications. The environment is highly collaborative and innovation-driven, with a strong emphasis on applied AI, production readiness, and measurable impact. You will work across diverse use cases including forecasting, geospatial analysis, and intelligent decision systems. ...
# Principal Machine Learning Data Scientist - Gen AI
...
Posted Jun 17, 2026
...
Job Description: • Xometry (NASDAQ: XMTR) powers the industries of today and tomorrow by connecting the people with big ideas to the manufacturers who can bring them to life. • Xometry’s digital marketplace gives manufacturers the critical resources they need to grow their business while also making it easy for buyers at Fortune 1000 companies to tap into global manufacturing capacity. • Xometry is seeking a Principal Data & ML Scientist to join our Generative AI team. • The ideal candidate will have a passion for advancing machine learning and generative AI capabilities, particularly for fine-tuning generative and language models, multimodal document understanding, and structured data extraction. • This person will leverage their expertise in generative models and data science to develop and optimize innovative AI-driven solutions that enhance Xometry's service offerings. • Provide technical leadership to the Generative AI team, setting technical direction, defining best practices, and ensuring the team follows industry standards in AI and ML development. • Lead strategic planning and roadmap development for generative AI initiatives, identifying high-impact projects and aligning them with Xometry’s business objectives. • Develop and deploy generative AI models and large language models (LLMs) for multimodal document processing, focusing on extracting structured data from technical drawings, purchasing orders, and other complex documents. • Lead the exploration and development of innovative text and image-based data processing solutions, including training and fine-tuning generative and language models. • Design and implement efficient workflows for data preparation, cleaning, and augmentation to support the training of generative AI models. • Utilize cloud platforms (e.g., Amazon Web Services) for large-scale data processing, model training, and deployment. • Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including engineering and business teams, to align generative AI solutions with business needs and drive impactful applications. • Mentor and guide team members on advanced machine learning techniques, model architecture design, and problem-solving strategies to elevate the team’s technical capabilities. • Continuously experiment and iterate on model performance, tuning architectures and parameters to improve accuracy and efficiency in a fast-paced, agile environment. • Stay updated with the latest research in generative AI, deep learning, and multimodal data processing, incorporating best practices and advancements into model development. Requirements: • A bachelor’s degree is required, but an advanced degree (M.S. or PhD) in computer science, machine learning, AI, or a related field is highly preferred. • 7+ years of experience in data science and machine learning, focusing on generative models, LLMs, or computer vision. • Expertise in large-scale language and vision models (e.g., Transformers, GPT, VLMs). • Experience with multimodal data processing (e.g., combining text, image, and 3D data). • Proficient in Python, including key libraries such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, pandas, and numpy. • Strong background in probability, statistics, and optimization techniques relevant to generative modeling. • Familiarity with cloud computing resources and tools for model training and deployment (e.g., AWS SageMaker). • Familiar with software engineering principles, including version control, reproducibility, and continuous integration. • Experience in the manufacturing, supply chain, or similar industries is a plus. Benefits:

results.title: LLM Engineer job with VIB | 12860950
highlights:
LLM Engineer job with VIB | 12860950
...
# LLM Engineer
...
Employer VIB Location Leuven Closing date 19 Aug 2026
...
VIB is a leading life sciences research institute in Flanders, Belgium, with a strong focus on translating basic scientific discoveries into benefits for society. With the launch of VIB.AI, we are expanding our mission to harness the power of artificial intelligence for life sciences research, innovation, and impact.
...
The VIB Center for Neuroscience Leuven (CNL) investigates the mechanisms of brain function and neurological disease across the scales of biology, spanning neurodegeneration, neurodevelopment, synaptic biology, and computational neuroscience. AI and large language models have transformative potential across these research areas.
...
We are now looking for an LLM Engineer in a shared position between VIB.AI's Machine Learning Expertise Unit and the Center for Neuroscience. You will split your time between building institute-wide AI tools and applying them directly to neuroscience research challenges.
...
As an LLM Engineer in this shared position, you will:
...
- Develop and deploy LLM-based pilot projects tailored to neuroscience research at the CNL, applying AI to questions in molecular, cellular and circuit neuroscience, neurodegeneration, neurodevelopment and disease mechanisms.
...
- Build and maintain components of VIB's institutional intelligence layer, including agentic bioinformatics workflows, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems, and AI-assisted experimental design tools.
...
- Design, implement, and evaluate LLM-based solutions for scientific text mining, multimodal integration of literature and biological data, and knowledge extraction to support discovery.
...
- Collaborate with wet-lab and computational neuroscience researchers to identify opportunities where generative AI and LLMs can accelerate scientific progress.
...
- Work with the Machine Learning Expertise Unit and VIB's AI Studio to translate pilot projects into practical tools that serve the broader VIB community.
...
- Promote ethical, transparent, and responsible use of LLMs in line with best practices and international standards.
...
- Mine opportunities and data at the center’s advanced technology expertise centers and affiliated central core facilities.
...
- Leverage VIB's Data Core services, including high-performance computing pipelines and large-scale GPU resources, to scale LLM development and deployment.
...
- PhD in machine learning, computer science, computational biology, computational neuroscience, statistics, or a related field.
- Strong experience with large language models, including pretraining, fine-tuning, prompt engineering, and evaluation.
- Knowledge of modern deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, JAX, TensorFlow).
- Experience with real-world applications of LLMs in scientific or biomedical contexts is a plus.
...
- Familiarity with neuroscience data types (imaging, electrophysiology, genomics, proteomics, or clinical data) is a plus but not required.
...
- Ability to work collaboratively across disciplines and communicate effectively with researchers from different backgrounds.
- A strong interest in applying LLMs to impactful life science and neuroscience problems.
...
- The opportunity to pioneer applications of LLMs in neuroscience and the broader life sciences.
...
- Direct access to VIB's Data Core services, which provide expert support in data management and high-performance computing, including optimized pipelines and large-scale GPU resources.
...
Please complete the application through the VIB online application tool. A complete application file should contain the following documents:
...
